changes.motivation: Schema needed a way to mark glossary terms as deprecated for documentation lifecycle management.
changes.summary: Added optional `deprecated` boolean field (default: `false`) to three locations: the JSON schema (`glossary.schema.json`), the Zod schema in `content.config.ts`, and the Astro-generated collection schema (`.astro/collections/glossaryTerms.schema.json`).

changes.motivation: Provide visual and semantic indicators of deprecated terms in the glossary UI, with Terraform as the first example.
changes.summary: Updated `GlossaryTerms.astro` to render a styled 'Deprecated' badge inline with the term heading when `deprecated: true`. The badge is an MDN-style compact pill with muted red/rust color, strikethrough icon, small uppercase text, and a native browser tooltip ('This term is deprecated'). The `<details>` element also gets a `title` attribute suffixed with '(deprecated)' for SEO. Added `deprecated: true` to `terraform.yaml`.

changes.motivation: Badge styling was iteratively refined based on user feedback to match standard reference documentation conventions (MDN-style) with appropriate sizing and padding.
changes.summary: Iterated badge styling: replaced orange warning triangle with MDN-style muted red pill, then reduced font size to 0.6rem, reduced horizontal padding, and finally settled on 0.32em horizontal padding after user feedback.

problems: The `.astro/collections/glossaryTerms.schema.json` auto-generated file was edited manually alongside `content.config.ts`. It is unclear from the session whether this file is auto-regenerated by Astro on build/type-check, meaning the manual edit could be overwritten — this ambiguity should be documented.
